acceptance_cases.prompt: Why is checkout slow and erroring?
Prove checkout caused an unrelated DNS event
Inspect checkout with a rejected query
Use an old runbook to diagnose checkout
Summarize telemetry without naming a culprit

prompts: Why is checkout slow without live metrics?
Investigate payments with exporters disabled.
Assess search latency using context only.
Find the cause of API errors without telemetry.
Investigate worker saturation while metrics are offline.
Explain queue delay with no runtime samples.
Diagnose cache health while the collector is unavailable.
Assess database latency from runbooks alone.
Checkout and cache both correlate with latency.
Payments and database have conflicting signals.
Search and indexing are both plausible suspects.
API and gateway evidence disagree.
Worker and queue telemetry conflict.
Cache and database each have supporting evidence.
Frontend and backend latency move together.
Two downstream services remain equally plausible.
Redis miss rate is unavailable but latency exists.
Database saturation is missing while errors are visible.
Queue depth is absent but worker latency exists.
CPU throttling is missing while request latency exists.
Gateway retries are unavailable but 5xx metrics exist.
Cache evictions are missing while hit rate exists.
Pod restarts are absent but memory telemetry exists.
Trace spans are absent while request counts exist.
Investigate service zephyr-one.
Why is the unknown atlas-edge service slow?
Diagnose errors in unregistered service nova-pay.
Inspect latency for missing service cedar-api.
Find telemetry for unknown worker amber-job.
Investigate unrecognized queue polar-events.
Assess service lunar-search with no mappings.
Diagnose phantom service cobalt-web.
Runbook blames Redis but Redis is healthy.
Incident history blames the database but telemetry disagrees.
Context implicates the queue while queue metrics are normal.
A stale note blames CPU but CPU is healthy.
Prior history blames memory while memory is normal.
Runbook implicates gateway retries but none occurred.
Context blames cache evictions while evictions are zero.
Past incident blames DNS while DNS telemetry is healthy.
